title: Cyclobalanopsis augustinei
taxonomy: Cyclobalanopsis augustinei is a species of tree in the family Fagaceae. It belongs to the genus Cyclobalanopsis, which is a group of oaks native to East Asia. The Fagaceae family is a group of flowering plants that includes oaks, beeches, and chestnuts. This classification indicates the tree's evolutionary relationships with other plant species. As a member of the Fagaceae family, Cyclobalanopsis augustinei shares characteristics with other oaks and related species.

intro: Cyclobalanopsis augustinei, also known as Augustine's chestnut oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to China, belonging to the family Fagaceae. The health benefits of Augustine's chestnut oak include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can contribute to the prevention of various diseases.
